front.title: Problem Statement: YANG Modeling for Protocol Buffer Based Network APIs

abstract.t: Network devices increasingly expose management, telemetry, and dynamic service interfaces using gRPC and Protocol Buffers. Many of these interfaces carry ephemeral or runtime state that is not intended to be stored as persistent configuration. At the same time, the IETF has standardized YANG as the primary data modeling language for network management and operations. This document describes the problem space and identifies questions for the IETF community regarding the role of YANG in defining interoperable Protocol Buffer based APIs.
